title: THX
content:
{{Top|rwc}} {{Otheruses}} {{Company |image=[[File:THX.svg]] |name=THX |founder=*[[George Lucas]] *[[Tomlinson Holman]] |ledby= |associations= |parent=* [[Lucasfilm]]
(1983–2002) * Creative Technology
(2002–2016) * Razer Inc.
(2016–present) |subsid= |headquarters=[[Wikipedia:San Rafael, California|San Rafael, California]] |locations= |role= |products= |founding=May 20, 1983 |dissolved= |affiliation=}} '''THX''' is a certification that is used to ensure a standard for high-fidelity sound reproduction systems(initially for theatrical movie theaters, screening rooms, home theaters, computer speakers, gaming consoles, and car audio systems). THX was developed by [[George Lucas]]' company [[Lucasfilm]], in [[1983]] to ensure that the soundtrack for the third ''[[Star Wars]]'' film, [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']], would be accurately reproduced in the best venues. In 2002, the THX division of Lucasfilm was spun off as a separate company. During the same year, THX was owned by sound card manufacturer [[Wikipedia:Creative Technology Limited|Creative Technology Limited]], which held a 60% share of the company. The company itself has had a long history with Creative, which was responsible for the creation of the first THX-certified audio card for computers, the [[Wikipedia:Sound Blaster Audigy|Sound Blaster Audigy 2]]. In 2016, THX was acquired by video-game hardware company [[Wikipedia:Razer Inc.|Razer Inc.]], with Razer owning all of THX and its intellectual property The name ''THX'' is a [[1138 (easter egg)|reference]] to George Lucas' first feature film, [[Wikipedia:THX 1138|''THX 1138'']]. ==Logo appearances== ===Amazing Life (2007)=== *[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace]] (2011 Blu-Ray) *[[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones]] (2011 Blu-Ray) *[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith]] (2011 Blu-Ray) *[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|Star Wars: The Clone Wars]] (2008 DVD) ===Broadway (1984)=== *[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace]] (2000 VHS) *[[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones]] (2002 VHS) *[[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope]] (1993 Laserdisc; 1995 VHS; 1995 Laserdisc; 1997 VHS; 2000 VHS; 2011 Blu-Ray) *[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back]] (1993 Laserdisc; 1995 VHS; 1995 Laserdisc; 1997 VHS; 2000 VHS; 2011 Blu-Ray) *[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi]] (1993 Laserdisc; 1995 VHS; 1995 Laserdisc; 1997 VHS; 2000 VHS; 2011 Blu-Ray) ===Broadway 2000 (1999)=== *[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace]] (1999 Theaters) ===Cavalcade (2001)=== *[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace]] (2001 DVD) *[[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones]] (2002 DVD) *[[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope]] (2004 DVD) *[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back]] (2004 DVD) *[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi]] (2004 DVD) *[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One]] (2005 DVD) ===Cimarron (1988)=== *[[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope]] (1997 Laserdisc) *[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back]] (1997 Laserdisc) *[[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi]] (1997 Laserdisc) ===Tex 2: Moo Can (1997)=== *[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume Two]] (2005 DVD) ===The Science of Sensation (2005)=== *[[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith]] (2005 VHS [International prints only];
